Campaign — Bloom Festival

For Bloom 2024's edition — 'Roots & Frequencies' — I art-directed the entire visual campaign from concept through to environmental installation.

How it is now.

A generative visual system inspired by root networks and sound waves — organic forms that could be algorithmically varied to create unique outputs for every poster and social tile. The colour palette shifts across a gradient spectrum, giving each event its own identity while maintaining cohesion.

How it was then.

The festival needed a campaign identity that felt fresh and exciting to a young creative audience, while remaining flexible enough to work across posters, social media, merchandise, wayfinding, and large-scale environmental installations. The budget was modest.

Want to dive into the details?

The generative visual system

We built a parametric tool that varies organic root-and-wave forms algorithmically — producing unique outputs for every poster and social tile while maintaining visual cohesion across the campaign. No two assets were identical.

Environmental graphics direction

Large-scale installations transformed the festival grounds into an immersive extension of the campaign. Wayfinding, stage backdrops, and interactive light installations all drew from the same generative system, creating a unified sensory experience.
